frameworks.name: mental physics engine / forward model of dynamics
frameworks.one_line: Brain simulates physics by encoding abstract physical variables like mass.

frameworks.name: motion-pattern analysis without physical-variable encoding
frameworks.one_line: Alternative account predicting motion without representing latent physics.

core_question: Does the human brain represent abstract physical variables such as mass, rather than only surface motion patterns, when understanding object interactions?
key_assumptions: Intuitive physical inference recruits specific parietal/frontal regions identified in prior work
Mass representations should generalize across irrelevant surface features if abstract
manipulations: Scenario varied across object interactions indicating mass
Task focus (action-free viewing; orthogonal color task)
key_findings: fMRI decoding recovered mass representations in physics-inference regions
Mass representations generalized across scenario, material, friction, and motion energy
Invariant mass coding emerged without action planning and under an orthogonal color task
interpretations: Parietal and frontal cortex encode abstract physical variables as inputs to a forward dynamics model akin to a physics engine
